# Zapstore

> Permissionless Android app store built on Nostr. Developers sign releases with a Nostr keypair, users discover apps through a social web of trust, and all payments flow over Bitcoin Lightning. No registration, no government ID, no fees, no gatekeepers.

- [Docs: Publishing apps](https://zapstore.dev/docs/publish)
- [Docs: Trust model](https://zapstore.dev/docs/trust-model)
- [Docs: FAQ](https://zapstore.dev/docs/faq)
- [Source code](https://github.com/zapstore)
- [Blog: Can Nostr fix app distribution?](https://zapstore.dev/blog/can-nostr-fix-app-distribution)
- [NIP-01: Basic protocol](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nostr-protocol/nips/refs/heads/master/01.md)
- [NIP-57: Lightning Zaps](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nostr-protocol/nips/refs/heads/master/57.md)
- [NIP-47: Nostr Wallet Connect](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nostr-protocol/nips/refs/heads/master/47.md)
- [NIP-50: Search](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nostr-protocol/nips/refs/heads/master/50.md)

## Relay

The canonical data source is a Nostr relay:

```
wss://relay.zapstore.dev
```

All data queries use standard NIP-01 filters over WebSocket. The relay also supports NIP-50 full-text search.

## Event Kinds

Zapstore uses these Nostr event kinds:

| Kind | Name | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| 32267 | Software Application | App metadata: name, description, icon, screenshots, platforms, repository, license |
| 30063 | Software Release | Release version, changelog, links to asset events via `e` tags |
| 3063 | Software Asset | Binary metadata: SHA-256 hash, download URL, platform, size, APK certificate hash, version code |
| 30509 | Identity Proof | Cryptographic link between a Nostr pubkey and an APK signing certificate (SPKI) |
| 30267 | App Stack | Curated list of apps. `a` tags reference kind 32267 events |
| 1111 | Comment | App reviews and comments, scoped to an app via `A` tag |
| 9735 | Zap Receipt | Lightning payment receipts for developer tips |
| 0 | Profile | Developer profiles |

## Resolution Chain

To go from "find me an app" to a verified binary, resolve this chain:

### 1. Find apps (kind 32267)

Filter by platform — only Android arm64 is currently supported:

```json
{"kinds": [32267], "#f": ["android-arm64-v8a"]}
```

To search by text (NIP-50):

```json
{"kinds": [32267], "#f": ["android-arm64-v8a"], "search": "maps"}
```

To look up a specific app by identifier:

```json
{"kinds": [32267], "#d": ["app.organicmaps"]}
```

The event contains tags for: name (`name`), description (`description`), icon URL (`icon`), repository URL (`repository`), license (`license`), website (`url`), and platforms (`f`).

### 2. Get the latest release (kind 30063)

Filter by the app author's pubkey and the app identifier:

```json
{"kinds": [30063], "authors": ["<app-author-pubkey>"], "#i": ["app.organicmaps"]}
```

Returns release events sorted by `created_at`. Each release has a version tag and `e` tags pointing to asset events.

### 3. Resolve assets (kind 3063)

Fetch the asset events referenced by the release's `e` tags:

```json
{"ids": ["<asset-event-id-1>", "<asset-event-id-2>"]}
```

Each asset event contains:
- `x` tag: SHA-256 hash of the binary
- `url` tag: download URL (typically `https://cdn.zapstore.dev/<sha256>`)
- `f` tag: platform identifier (e.g., `android-arm64-v8a`)
- `size` tag: file size in bytes
- `version_code` tag: Android version code

### 4. Verify developer identity (kind 30509)

Look up the identity proof linking the developer's Nostr pubkey to their APK signing certificate:

```json
{"kinds": [30509], "authors": ["<app-author-pubkey>"]}
```

This event cryptographically proves the developer controls both the Nostr key and the APK signing key.

### 5. Browse curated lists (kind 30267)

```json
{"kinds": [30267]}
```

Each App Stack event contains `a` tags pointing to kind 32267 app events. Curators are identified by their Nostr pubkey.

### 6. Read reviews (kind 1111)

Comments scoped to an app:

```json
{"kinds": [1111], "#A": ["32267:<app-author-pubkey>:<app-d-tag>"]}
```

## Publishing

Install the `zsp` CLI tool:

```bash
go install github.com/zapstore/zsp@latest
```

The fastest path is the interactive wizard:

```bash
zsp publish --wizard
```

Or publish directly from a repository URL:

```bash
zsp publish -r github.com/your-org/your-app
```

The wizard creates a `zapstore.yaml` config. Commit it to your repo for automatic relay whitelisting.

### Example `zapstore.yaml`

```yaml
name: Amethyst
description: The all-in-one Nostr client
license: MIT
builder: npub142gywvjkq0dv6nupggyn2euhx4nduwc7yz5f24ah9rpmunr2s39se3xrj0
repository: https://github.com/vitorpamplona/amethyst
homepage: https://amethyst.social/
assets:
  - amethyst-googleplay.*.apk
remote_metadata:
  - github
```

Real examples: [Amethyst](https://github.com/vitorpamplona/amethyst/blob/main/zapstore.yaml), [Nostrmo](https://github.com/haorendashu/nostrmo/blob/main/zapstore.yaml)

### Signing

Set `SIGN_WITH` before publishing:

| Method | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Nostr private key | `SIGN_WITH=nsec1...` |
| NIP-46 bunker | `SIGN_WITH=bunker://pubkey?relay=...&secret=...` |
| Browser extension | `SIGN_WITH=browser` |

For CI/CD, use a bunker URL stored as a secret.

### Metadata enrichment

`zsp` pulls metadata from external sources automatically:

```yaml
metadata_sources:
  - playstore
  - github
  - fdroid
```

## Desktop CLI

Install the desktop package manager:

```bash
go install github.com/zapstore/zapstore@latest
```

Commands:

```bash
zapstore search <query>        # discover apps (supports --json)
zapstore install <app-id>      # fetch, verify, install
zapstore update [<app-id>]     # update one or all
zapstore list                  # show installed (supports --json)
zapstore remove <app-id>       # uninstall
```

## Payments

All payments use Bitcoin Lightning via the Nostr protocol:

- **Zaps (NIP-57):** Users tip developers directly. Developers receive 100% — no platform cut.
- **Wallet connection (NIP-47):** Nostr Wallet Connect enables wallet-to-app communication for payments.

## Custom NIP Specs

- [Software Releases](https://nostrhub.io/naddr1qvzqqqrcvypzq7xwd748yfjrsu5yuerm56fcn9tntmyv04w95etn0e23xrczvvraqy2hwumn8ghj7un9d3shjtnyd968gmewwp6kyqqzvy6sjzn058)
- [Software Artifact Attestations](https://nostrhub.io/naddr1qvzqqqrcvypzq7xwd748yfjrsu5yuerm56fcn9tntmyv04w95etn0e23xrczvvraqy2hwumn8ghj7un9d3shjtnyd968gmewwp6kyqqavde8jur5danhyctsdp5kxttfv3jkuarfw3uj6urjdahkvuc2wg0mz)
- [App Curation](https://nostrhub.io/naddr1qvzqqqrcvypzq7xwd748yfjrsu5yuerm56fcn9tntmyv04w95etn0e23xrczvvraqy2hwumn8ghj7un9d3shjtnyd968gmewwp6kyqqz8qeqp2qk5p)
- [Identity Verification](https://nostrhub.io/naddr1qvzqqqrcvypzq7xwd748yfjrsu5yuerm56fcn9tntmyv04w95etn0e23xrczvvraqy2hwumn8ghj7un9d3shjtnyd968gmewwp6kyqqvv9nk2mn594ekk6tvd3esftqagq)
